title: Tuesday Stories & Stations (0-5 years)
location: Library Meeting Room, 2nd Floor, Centre Square Mall
description: Stories, songs and rhymes followed by caregiver-and-child activity stations. June 16, 2:00-3:30 p.m.
notes: Final scheduled session in the current series. Public library welcomes newcomers.

title: Wednesday Story Time (2-4 years)
location: Library Meeting Room, 2nd Floor, Centre Square Mall
description: Free stories, fun and crafts with a parent or caregiver. June 17, 10:00-11:00 a.m.
notes: Final scheduled session in the current series. Dayhomes welcome.

title: Friday Baby Time (0-18 months)
location: Library Meeting Room, 2nd Floor, Centre Square Mall
description: Free early-literacy program with music, rhyme and lap play for babies and caregivers. June 19, 10:00-11:00 a.m.
notes: Siblings welcome. Final scheduled session in the current series.

title: 38th Annual Bike Rodeo
description: Free bike-themed afternoon for children in Kindergarten through Grade 6 and their families. June 20, 12:00-4:00 p.m.
notes: Bring a bike and helmet. Admission is free.

title: Under the Sea: YPL Summer Reading Club
description: Weekly crafts, games, activities and stories, plus a summer reading log and prizes. Tuesday and Thursday sessions.
notes: Registration required by phone at (867) 920-5642 or email Library@yellowknife.ca.

title: Canada Day 2026
description: Free community Canada Day celebration at Somba K'e Civic Plaza. Detailed schedule is still to be announced.
notes: All-day event; check official page again when the schedule is published.

title: Tennis NWT Summer Camp (Ages 8-12)
description: Tennis NWT Summer Camps are designed for beginner and intermediate youth players ages 8'12. Led by Tennis Canada'certified coaches, these camps introduce young players to the fundamentals of tennis in a fun, supportive, and positive environment. Camp activities focus on skill development, confidence building, and a love for the game - all while keeping things light and enjoyable. Time: 09:00 am - 12:00 pm.
notes: FULL - Waitlist Available when checked June 14, 2026. Course ID 22225; 5 sessions.

title: City Summer Camp (Ages 5-6)- Week One
description: Camp is a fun way for children to spend their Summer break. It is also a place to make a positive impact in children's lives through the help of our camp leaders. The City of Yellowknife Summer Camp activities provide children with the skills in participation, forming friendships and socialization abilities. With successful implementation of camp leaders, campers will gain self-esteem and self-confidence through experiences and adventures at camp. Camp will be held at a number of City facilities including the Aquatic Centre, Library, Multiplex, & Fieldhouse. A list of daily drop off, and pick up locations will be provided to registrants in the days before camp starts. Time: 08:00 am - 05:00 pm.
notes: 22 spots left when checked June 14, 2026. Course ID 21496; 4 sessions.

title: Kids Golf Camps ' 3-Day Program (5yrs - 8yrs)
description: Get your junior golfer out on the course and building skills this season! Our 3-day camps are designed to be fun, active, and educational, with a focus on developing the fundamentals of the game. Throughout the camp, we'll cover: Full swing with irons and driver. Chipping and putting techniques. On-course experience playing a hole. Perfect for young golfers of all skill levels looking to learn, improve, and enjoy the game in a supportive environment. Time: 03:15 pm - 04:00 pm.
notes: FULL - Waitlist Available when checked June 14, 2026. Course ID 22461; 3 sessions.

title: 2026 Garden Competition
description: Residents can submit garden photos, browse entries and vote. Categories include tiny, bee-friendly, vegetable, flower, container and rock gardens.
notes: App access and voting details are still to be announced.

title: New Year's Eve Fireworks
location: Somba K'e Civic Plaza / Frame Lake viewing area
description: Annual City fireworks display viewed from Somba K'e Civic Plaza and designated areas around Frame Lake.
notes: The 2026 date is listed, but the exact 2026 time has not yet been published.

title: Yellowknife Pride Parade & Pride in the Park
description: Fourth annual Pride Parade at 12:00 p.m., followed by an afternoon celebration with free BBQ, entertainment and a vendor market.
notes: Public Facebook Page post published June 14, 2026.

title: Yellowknife Public Library Summer Book Club
description: Summer book club meets on the last Tuesday of each month. The upcoming read is On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous by Ocean Vuong.
notes: Exact meeting time, age suitability and cost were not stated. Dates reflect the last Tuesdays of June through August 2026.

title: Yellowknife Fire Division Open House
description: Fire-safety open house with fire truck rides, a mini combat challenge, fire extinguisher training and hands-on activities for all ages.
notes: Held June 13, 2026 from 1:00-4:00 p.m. Venue address and cost were not stated in the post.

title: Learn How to Rollerski - Track Attack
description: Introductory rollerski session for Track Attack skiers. Rollerskis are provided.
notes: June 14, 2026 at 1:30 p.m. Confirm Track Attack eligibility, cost, helmet requirements and exact meeting point.

title: Summer Skating Season Kickstart Camp
description: On-ice and off-ice summer camp with sessions for CanSkate, Star Prep and Star skaters. Open to skaters in the Northwest Territories and Nunavut.
notes: Registration is open. Exact ages, session times, fees and equipment requirements were not included in the Facebook text.

title: Bike for Books
description: Community bike ride with prizes, snacks, hotdogs and games afterward. Participants could join a fundraising team or donate.
notes: June 13, 2026: meet at the Fieldhouse at 10:30 a.m. or Somba K'e Park at 11:30 a.m. Participation cost was not stated.

title: Hide Camp Story Walk
description: A self-guided Hide Camp Story Walk announced by the NWT Literacy Council.
notes: Facebook post said the Story Walk started June 9, 2026. Location, end date, ages and cost were not stated in the accessible post text.

title: 2222 Hi-RezNDN Exhibit Opening Reception
provider: Prince of Wales Northern Heritage Centre
location: Museum Cafe, Prince of Wales Northern Heritage Centre
description: Opening reception for 2222 Hi-RezNDN: The Cyberpunk Smoke Signals of Morgan Zoe. Light refreshments will be served.
notes: June 25, 2026 from 7:00-8:30 p.m. Age suitability and cost were not explicitly stated.

title: Old Town Ramble & Ride 20th Anniversary Festival
description: Three-day community festival featuring music, art, food, culture and family fun, including a children's play area.
notes: Festival dates verified from public Facebook Page posts. Admission cost and detailed schedule were not stated.

title: Old Town Ramble & Ride Volunteer Opportunities
description: Volunteer roles include stage and signage setup and helping at the children's play area. Volunteers receive a T-shirt and BBQ.
notes: Minimum age, shift length and accessibility details were not stated.

title: Yellowknife School Garden Summer Volunteers
description: Volunteer to water, weed and care for Yellowknife school gardens while students are on summer vacation.
notes: The post invited summer volunteers but did not state exact dates, schedule or minimum age.

title: NWT Judo Day - Free Drop-In
location: Yellowknife Judo Club, basement of Quality Inn & Suites, 5010 49 St
description: Free drop-in judo event for all ages and experience levels, led by Olympian Jean Pierre Cantin. No prior experience is required and uniforms are supplied.
notes: June 27, 2026 from 10:00 a.m.-3:00 p.m. Participants may drop in at any time.

title: 2026 Yellowknife Multisport Judo Summer Camp
description: Five-week multisport camp combining judo with swimming, arts and crafts, hiking, dodgeball, soccer and other activities. No prior judo experience is required.
notes: Ages 7-12. Drop-off 8:00-9:00 a.m.; pickup 4:00-5:00 p.m. $230 is the four-day first-week price; verify prices for later weeks during registration. Campers need indoor/outdoor shoes, lunch, snacks, water, swimwear, towel and change of clothes.

title: Le Petit Junior Tennis Clinic
description: Weekly introductory tennis clinic for children ages 3-5, Saturdays from 9:00-9:45 a.m. Club racquets are available to borrow.
notes: Runs through the end of August. $75 seasonal Le Petit membership includes the clinic; no separate clinic fee.

title: Beginner Children's Tennis Clinic
description: Coach-led beginner tennis clinic for children ages 6-10, Mondays and Wednesdays from 6:00-7:00 p.m. Loaner racquets are available.
notes: Runs from May 18 through the end of August. $100 seasonal children/youth membership includes clinics; no separate clinic fee.

title: Youth Intermediate Tennis Clinic
description: Coach-led intermediate clinic for youth ages 10-16, Mondays and Wednesdays from 7:00-8:00 p.m. Loaner racquets are available.
notes: Runs from May 18 through the end of August. $100 seasonal children/youth membership includes clinics; contact the club if unsure about skill level.

title: Youth High Performance Tennis Clinic
description: High-performance youth tennis training for ages 10-16, Mondays and Wednesdays from 8:00-9:00 p.m. Loaner racquets are available.
notes: Runs from May 18 through the end of August. $100 seasonal children/youth membership includes clinics; intended for advanced youth players.

title: Basketball NWT Golf Tournament Fundraiser
description: Team golf tournament with green fees, banquet meal, prizes and contests. Funds support Basketball NWT U15 and U17 boys teams attending the 2026 National Championships.
notes: July 17, 2026 with a 1:00 p.m. shotgun start. $400 per team. Participant age requirements, team size and registration deadline were not stated; register or inquire at Golf.bnwt@gmail.com or 867-446-6016.

title: Yellowknife Table Tennis Drop-In Sessions
description: Weekly table tennis drop-in sessions for practice, friendly rallies and games with experienced players. Tuesdays 7:00-8:30 p.m. and Saturdays 3:00-5:00 p.m.
notes: The published season ended May 30, 2026. Free for students; adults were $10 per drop-in. Monitor the Facebook Page for summer or next-season dates. Equipment availability and student age range were not stated.

title: Sundogs Soccer League - Summer 2026 Youth Divisions
location: Indoor and outdoor venues around Yellowknife
description: Summer youth soccer league with players placed into divisions and teams by age and/or gender. Games are held at indoor and outdoor venues around Yellowknife.
notes: Expected to run from early June to early August 2026; exact schedule was not posted. Standard fee is $50. Current YBSC youth players born in 2018 or earlier are included at no extra cost; 2025/26 AMSL players receive 50% off. Select 2019-born players may be accepted. NWT Soccer membership is also required.

title: Yellowknife Bay Soccer Club Sundogs Development Program 2025/26
description: Year-round developmental soccer program covering the winter indoor season and following summer outdoor season. Groups range from Sunpups/U3 through U18, with mixed and boys/girls divisions depending on age.
notes: No tryouts; the club emphasizes player development, attendance, effort and positive behaviour. New players are accepted when group space is available. Confirm current fees, schedule and openings at ybsc_registrations@yellowknifebaysoccer.ca. Annual cycle is October to October.

title: Yellowknife Gymnastics 2026 Summer Camp
description: Weekly full-day gymnastics camp with apparatus training, crafts, games, outdoor activities and supervised free play. Open to all skill levels.
notes: Eight camp weeks from June 29-August 21, 2026. $371 per week. Week 1 closes July 1 and Week 6 closes August 3. Child must be age 5 during the camp week.

title: WAG Competitive Team Tryouts
description: Annual Women's Artistic Gymnastics competitive team tryouts. Ages 6-8 attend June 22-23; ages 9+ attend June 24-25, from 4:30-6:30 p.m.
notes: Athletes must attend both assigned days. Registration is required; no walk-ins. Limit of 10 athletes per age range.

title: Yellowknife Gymnastics All-Ages Drop-In
description: Supervised all-ages gymnastics drop-in on Fridays and Saturdays from 7:00-8:00 p.m. Children age 6 and younger need a parent or guardian on the floor.
notes: Under age 1 is free; age 1+ is $15. Walk-ins depend on available space. Low-sensory and preschool drop-ins are also offered.

title: Northwestel Outdoor Soccer & Basketball Summer Camp
description: Seven-week summer camp combining soccer and basketball skill development, teamwork, friendly competition and active play.
notes: Runs June 29-August 14, 2026 for ages 5-12. Weekly schedule, locations, fee and available spaces must be confirmed in the RAMP registration system.

title: KidSport NWT Grant
description: Confidential financial assistance for sport registration fees and equipment so children and youth can participate in an organized season of sport.
notes: For NWT children and youth age 18 and under facing financial barriers. Application is ongoing; contact tylerrent@sportnorth.com with questions.

title: Sport North Rising Star Athlete Funding
description: Funding assistance for young athletes attending eligible NWT sport programs or Canadian skill-development and technical-training camps.
notes: Approximate target ages vary by development stage: about 6-9 for fundamentals, 8/9 through early adolescence for Learn to Train, and roughly 11/12-16 for Train to Train camps. Applications must be submitted at least one month before the event.

title: Roger Walker Memorial Golf Tournament for KidSport NWT
description: Team golf tournament supporting KidSport NWT. Registration includes lunch, dinner, green fees, prizes and contests.
notes: July 10, 2026. Team registration is $400. Participation age and team composition were not stated; contact ecarey@sportnorth.com.

title: Maker Camp - July 27-31
description: A five-day creative maker camp for children ages 9-12, offering hands-on projects and opportunities to build creative skills.
notes: Runs July 27-31, 2026. Official listed fee is $400. Check the registration page for current space availability.

title: Maker Camp - August 17-21
description: A creative maker camp featuring paper mache, cyanotypes, e-textiles, printmaking, wood construction and painting projects.
notes: Runs August 17-21, 2026, 9:00 a.m.-4:00 p.m. Fee is $400. Campers bring lunch and snacks. Official page currently shows fully booked.

title: English Cafe - Tuesday Evening
description: Informal English conversation practice offering a welcoming place for newcomers and English learners to build confidence.
notes: Tuesday, June 16, 2026, 5:30-7:30 p.m. Contact anna@nwtliteracy.ca for participation details. No fee was stated on the official event listing.

title: English Cafe - Saturday Morning
description: Informal English conversation practice offering a welcoming place for newcomers and English learners to build confidence.
notes: Saturday, June 20, 2026, 10:30 a.m.-12:00 p.m. Contact anna@nwtliteracy.ca for participation details. No fee was stated on the official event listing.

title: English Cafe - Summer Saturday Series
description: A Saturday English conversation series for newcomers and English learners to practise speaking in a welcoming community setting.
notes: Saturdays from August 1 through September 26, 2026, 10:30 a.m.-12:00 p.m. Contact anna@nwtliteracy.ca. No fee was stated on the official listing.

title: Classic Movie Monday: The Daughter of Dawn
location: Yellowknife Public Library - 2nd Floor, Centre Square Mall
description: A free screening of the 1920 silent film The Daughter of Dawn for National Indigenous History Month. The film features an entirely Indigenous cast, including members of the Kiowa and Comanche Nations.
notes: June 15, 2026, 6:00-8:00 p.m. The official listing does not state an age restriction or registration requirement. Contact Yellowknife Public Library at (867) 920-5642.

title: Explore French-Language Summer Immersion Program
provider: Government of Canada / Council of Ministers of Education, Canada
description: A government-funded French immersion experience lasting approximately 19 to 33 days, with language classes, cultural activities, accommodation and meals at participating institutions.
notes: Eligible Canadian citizens and permanent residents can apply. The contribution generally covers tuition, accommodation, meals and program activities, but participants may pay registration fees, travel and personal expenses. Check immediately for 2026 wait-list or late-application availability and confirm each session's age requirements.

title: Destination Clic French First-Language Summer Program
provider: Government of Canada / Council of Ministers of Education, Canada
description: A 19- to 21-day summer enrichment experience for French-speaking students in Grades 8 to 10 who live outside Quebec, combining language, culture, recreation, accommodation and meals.
notes: The regular 2026 application deadline was February 24, 2026. Government funding covers most program costs, but travel, registration and personal expenses may remain. Record retained for wait-list enquiries and future-year planning.

title: Canadian Cadet Program and Summer Training
provider: Department of National Defence / Canadian Armed Forces
location: Local Cadet unit and assigned summer training centre
description: Free youth programming with leadership, citizenship, fitness and specialized activities such as aviation, outdoor skills, sailing, sports and music. Eligible cadets may attend summer training courses.
notes: Joining Cadets is free. Summer training selection normally requires membership in a local unit and may depend on prior training, available spaces and unit nomination. Contact a Yellowknife unit to confirm whether any 2026 options remain; otherwise join now to prepare for summer 2027.

title: Junior Canadian Rangers Program and Enhanced Training
provider: Department of National Defence / Canadian Armed Forces
location: Local Junior Canadian Ranger patrol and regional training locations
description: A free northern youth program combining traditional skills, outdoor safety, community service, leadership, physical activity and Ranger skills, with possible regional enhanced training opportunities.
notes: Uniforms, equipment and regular activities are provided without charge; approved travel, accommodation and meals for training are normally covered. Confirm whether a Yellowknife patrol currently accepts members and whether summer 2026 enhanced training has spaces.

title: Indigenous Summer Programs - Bold Eagle, Raven and Black Bear
description: Six- to eight-week paid summer programs combining Indigenous cultural learning with Canadian Armed Forces training, teamwork, fitness and leadership development.
notes: For eligible First Nations, Métis and Inuit applicants age 16+ who have completed at least Grade 10. Travel, accommodation, meals, clothing and equipment are covered and participants are paid. The usual application deadline was April 30, 2026; ask a recruiter whether late applications or vacancies remain. Bold Eagle is the most geographically relevant option for NWT applicants.
